$comment: OpenClaw config for a single-tenant per-client VM, backed by avots.ai. Strict JSON (no comments/trailing commas) so it parses everywhere. See README.md for the why behind every block. Pre-seeded at provision time into ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json. Secrets are NOT here: AVOTS_API_KEY and OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN come from ~/.openclaw/.env via ${VAR} refs.

models.$comment: mode:merge keeps OpenClaw's bundled provider catalogs and ADDS the avots provider. STEP 1 of 2: register the provider + its model so the model exists at runtime.

compat.$comment: TOOL CALLING: OpenClaw sends the tools parameter unless a model sets compat.supportsTools:false (capability is opt-OUT, permissive by default - see src/agents/model-tool-support.ts). We set it TRUE explicitly so the intent is recorded and so nobody flips it off by accident. supportsDeveloperRole is FORCED to false by OpenClaw at runtime for non-native openai-completions baseUrls anyway; we set it to match and avoid 400s.

tools.$comment: Tool policy is defense-in-depth on TOP of the sandbox. profile 'coding' gives fs/runtime/web/etc; we DENY the highest-blast-radius tools globally, and lock untrusted/unknown senders down to read-only via toolsBySender['*'].

elevated.$comment: Elevated exec BYPASSES the sandbox. Keep it OFF. If a trusted owner truly needs it, enable per channel+identity here, never globally.

defaults.$comment: STEP 2 of 2: the model registered above must ALSO be allowlisted here using the fully-qualified <provider-id>/<model-id> key. agents.defaults.models is an allowlist when set; agents.defaults.model.primary selects the active model. Both use 'avots/anthropic/claude-opus-4.8'.

sandbox.$comment: SECURITY-CRITICAL. mode:all sandboxes EVERY session (incl. the main one). Default backend = in-gateway isolation (no docker.sock needed). workspaceAccess:none = the sandbox cannot read/write the host workspace. To use the Docker backend instead, see README - it requires the docker CLI in the image and a NON-host docker context; do NOT mount the host socket.

channels.$comment: Messaging is OUTBOUND-ONLY (long-polling); no inbound webhook/TLS. Enable exactly ONE channel per client and lock it to the owner's identity. Tokens come from .env. Example below is Telegram; replace with the client's chosen channel during onboarding. allowFrom MUST be set so the agent only answers the owner.
